idea.topic: What if the Milky Way Collided
facts: The Andromeda galaxy is approaching us at 250,000 miles per hour
The collision would release energy equal to 100 billion Suns
The merged galaxy would contain over 1 trillion stars, spanning 200,000 light-years, roughly 1,000 times the distance from New York to LA
idea.payoff: and we still don't know what the new galaxy would look like
idea.title: Galactic Crash 🌌
idea.description: Imagine the Milky Way, our home galaxy, colliding with Andromeda in a cosmic crash 🌌. The universe has been setting up this collision for eons, with the two galaxies hurtling towards each other at breakneck speeds 🚀. Will we be around to witness this transformative event that will reshape the very fabric of our cosmic neighborhood 🌠?

scenes.text: WHAT IF THE MILKY WAY COLLIDED?
scenes.visual: Two distinct spiral galaxies (representing Milky Way and Andromeda) slowly appearing and subtly moving towards each other in deep space.

scenes.text: Andromeda approaches at 250,000 mph. Impact in 4 BILLION years!
scenes.visual: A detailed render of the Andromeda galaxy, slowly zooming in with a subtle indicator of speed and direction.
A stylized visualization of two galaxies merging, releasing a massive burst of bright, fiery energy outwards.

scenes.text: New galaxy: 1 TRILLION+ stars, 200,000 light-years wide.
scenes.visual: A vast, majestic rendering of a newly formed elliptical galaxy (Milkomeda), slowly rotating, emphasizing its immense scale.

scenes.text: But we still don't know what it will look like.
scenes.visual: A captivating, slightly ambiguous image of a potential merged galaxy, slowly morphing and swirling, with a subtle question mark overlay fading in and out.
